channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - Miami Heat
channel.description: Miami Heat trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: Shams Charania: Giannis Antetokounmpo is expected to tell the Bucks 'no' when asked to commit long-term
item.description: Antetokounmpo has one guaranteed year remaining on his contract, giving him considerable leverage over his next destination. The Miami Heat, Golden State Warriors, Los Angeles Lakers, Minnesota Timberwolves and Cleveland Cavaliers are among the teams expected to pursue him. "Any conversation that the Bucks are going to have with Giannis Antetokounmpo, from my understanding, is going to be a very simple one: 'Do you want to stay here long-term and commit long-term in June?'" Charania said on the

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.description: Antetokounmpo has one guaranteed year remaining on his contract, giving him considerable leverage over his next destination. The Miami Heat, Golden State Warriors, Los Angeles Lakers, Minnesota Timberwolves and Cleveland Cavaliers are among the teams expected to pursue him. "Any conversation that the Bucks are going to have with Giannis Antetokounmpo, from my understanding, is going to be a very simple one: 'Do you want to stay here long-term and commit long-term in June?'" Charania said on the

item.title: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft …
item.description: NBA: The results are in for picks 5-14 in the NBA Draft Lottery 2026: 5. LA Clippers 6. Brooklyn Nets 7. Sacramento Kings 8. Atlanta Hawks 9. Dallas Mavericks 10. Milwaukee Bucks 11. Golden State Warriors 12. Oklahoma City Thunder 13. Miami Heat 14. Charlotte Hornets

item.title: That aura, that respect, and what he commanded from us …
item.description: Dwyane Wade: Our situation was a little different because Stan was a young coach, a very good young coach. I thank Stan so much for my early success. But we’re talking about the Godfather. We’re talking about one of the greatest coaches of all time coming back. That aura, that respect, and what he commanded from us was just totally different. We had a bunch of grown-man knuckleheads. We needed to be brought in. We needed to be pulled together. We weren’t the most talented team, but we had a lot

item.title: Metta World Peace on Bam Adebayo’s 83-point game: ‘You can’t blame him for going for it, I would’ve done the same’
item.description: You were a teammate of Kobe Bryant, who had an 81-point performance prior to your arrival. Bam Adebayo recently surpassed this with an 83-point game. How impressive was that? Metta World Peace: I was very happy for him. I was super impressed. As I read more articles and heard a lot of the noise around it, it kind of diluted the emotion and excitement around it. It seemed like everyone was just talking about the free throws and the and-ones. He had 31 points in the first quarter. That’s crazy. At

item.title: Terry Rozier appeared in front of an arbitrator last …
item.description: The NBA and former Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier appeared in front of an arbitrator last month to again argue whether he should have been paid this season, three sources briefed on the hearing said. It is the second time the league and the player have asked an arbitrator to decide whether Rozier should receive his $26.6 million salary as he faces federal charges over his alleged involvement in a sport gambling scheme.

item.title: So, yes, you could hear about the framework of a …
item.description: Technically, the Heat isn’t eligible to trade its 2026 first-round pick (No. 13 overall) because NBA rules prevent teams from being without a first-round selection in two consecutive years (the Heat sent a top-14 protected first-round 2027 pick to the Charlotte Hornets in the Rozier trade). But the Heat could pick a player on behalf of another team at No. 13 in the first round of this year’s draft as part of a prearranged trade. This means the Heat and Bucks could agree to a trade prior to next

item.title: Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. the finalists for Social Justice Champion award
item.description: NBA: As we honor the sixth year of the NBA Social Justice Champion award, join us in recognizing Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. as the finalists for the 2025-26 season. This award honors NBA Hall of Famer Kareem Abdul-Jabbar and his lifetime commitment to driving impactful change around social justice.
